name: Old Nell
description: A wiry old woman with piercing blue eyes and wind-tangled grey hair. She leans on a gnarled walking stick.

start.text: Come to visit old Nell, have you? Not many do. I keep the light burning and I watch the sea. What brings you up here?
start.narrative: Nell peers at you from beneath wild eyebrows.

responses.text: What do you see from up here?

view.text: Everything. Ships coming and going. Storms rolling in. And late at night... boats that don't want to be seen, slipping into the cove east of here.
view.narrative: She points a bony finger toward the eastern shore.

smugglers.text: Smuggler's Cove, folk call it. They move goods by night — nothing too wicked, mostly. But lately there's been new faces. Harder sorts. They've been bringing crates marked with Eltharan runes.

runes.text: Aye. Same markings as on the ruins to the north. Someone's moving artefacts out of those ruins and shipping them gods-know-where. The harbour master knows, I'm sure of it. But he won't talk.
runes.narrative: She taps her stick on the floor for emphasis.
